case: Over the last few days my coworker in the office has had these huge mood swings. They get angry and shout or start crying for no obvious reason. I'm not sure if there's a mental illness behind it or if they might be on medication. I'm worried they might do something to hurt themselves or others but I'm also not sure if it's just a phase. I don't know how urgent it is. I'm a medical student, near the end of my final year. Help me figure out if we need immediate help or if there's something else we can try.
why_this_case: Triage of severe mood symptoms in another person

case: i had a baby 2 months ago i cry all the time no mental health clinic around me do i have postpartum depression

I've been doing therapy for my anxiety under the scottish nhs in glasgow but i'm getting worse and i'd like a referral to a psychiatrist soon are the wait times really long or is there a local mental health team to see sooner i'm worried i'll be stuck on a list for months and i want to know if there's a way to get help faster
why_this_case: Local service access and referral/wait time

case: i keep feeling worthless and sometimes i have suicidal thoughts but i'm not even sure if i really want to do anything

The specialist recommended a consistent bedtime at 9pm, some bedtime relaxation exercises, and occasional low-dose melatonin. case: I'm planning to stop my venlafaxine, I'm taking 225 mg but I'm not anxious anymore (thank god). It's just become pretty expensive and I usually do better in the summertime. Any therapist recommendations around the Toronto Danforth areas, specifically for anxiety?

I gave birth about a month ago and was diagnosed with postpartum depression. My doctor put me on a low dose of citalopram (10 mg) and I'm also doing weekly therapy sessions. I'm breastfeeding, have no allergies, and I'm not having any suicidal thoughts. I feel so exhausted I can hardly do the basic stuff at home, and it's been going on for weeks. Do you think it's normal, or should I discuss adjusting my medication with my doctor?
why_this_case: Medication decision in sensitive clinical context
